prompts.text: Distinguish letter name from letter sound: the letter is called 'ay' but makes the /a/ sound (as in apple). Name both each time: 'That's the letter A — it says /a/.'
When a letter appears, say it together — name, then sound — then find it nearby: 'That's B, it says /b/. Can you spot a B anywhere around us?'
Core question to return to: 'What sound does this letter make?' Knowing the sound is what leads to reading, not just the name.
Build connections: 'What word starts with [letter]?' Use familiar words first — their name, family names, favourite foods.
